key_strengths: World's largest offshore wind developer outside China with ~24% global ex-China market share and 30+ years of execution experience
10.2 GW European operating fleet generating ~DKK 25 billion annual EBITDA under long-term government CfD contracts and corporate PPAs
Revenue predictability from 20-25 year contracted power sales to electricity grids and large industrial counterparties
Danish state 50.1% ownership provides implicit sovereign backstop against capital markets disruptions
Post-rights-issue balance sheet repair and restored liquidity following DKK 60 billion rights issue in late 2025
key_risks: Construction execution on the 8.1 GW pipeline — repeating pattern of optimistic underwriting and impairments (DKK 50 billion cumulative 2023-2025)
Credit rating one notch above junk (BBB-) with FFO/debt at 13.9% vs S&P's 25% investment-grade floor
Residual US political risk and demonstrated vulnerability to regulatory/macroeconomic assumption failures
European auction pipeline deterioration — shut out of UK CfD Allocation Round 7 with zero awards while RWE won 6.9 GW
Insider CEO succession with no financial clawback and permanent structural discount from state ownership misalignment
red_flags: DKK 28.4 billion impairment in 2023 and additional DKK 15.6 billion in 2024 from a single geography (US)
ROIC of 1.67% against WACC of 7.4% — deeply sub-par, value-destroying capital allocation at the corporate level
Three successive credit downgrades from BBB+ to BBB- in three years
Reported net income zero or negative for multiple consecutive years — only adjusted earnings are meaningful
New CEO (Errboe) was Deputy CEO during the US expansion decisions; no clawback mechanism for those responsible
Business requires continuous capital markets access at scale — any credit deterioration creates acute funding risk
buy_triggers: Share price declines to DKK 100-110, providing genuine discount to base-case intrinsic value and shifting risk-reward to at least 3:1
2026 EBITDA delivered above DKK 28 billion guidance with no new construction impairments and S&P credit outlook revised to stable
Two or more major new European CfD contract awards in 2026 auctions confirming AR7 shutout was a pricing anomaly
sell_triggers: Any new impairment of DKK 5 billion or more on the 8.1 GW construction portfolio — confirms optimistic underwriting pattern has not been corrected
2026 EBITDA guidance revised materially downward, or any signal of an additional equity raise in 2026-2027
S&P downgrades Orsted to BB+ (sub-investment-grade), triggering forced bond selling and structurally higher funding costs
valuation_summary: At DKK 139, Orsted trades roughly at base-case fair value with an EV/EBITDA of ~9x on 2026 guidance; Morningstar's fair value of DKK 160 implies only ~15% upside while the discounted bear-case intrinsic value is DKK 62-91 per share, meaning the current price already sits above the pessimistic-but-plausible scenario with no meaningful margin of safety.
source_summary: Analysis based on web search results covering Orsted annual reports 2024-2025, Q4 2025 investor presentations, S&P Global Ratings, Morningstar research, analyst price targets from multiple financial data providers, and company announcements through February 2026.
